message:
Update CLAUDE.md with refactoring guidelines and pure Zig architecture

CRITICAL: Document file size limits and pure Zig architecture to prevent future issues.

File Size Guidelines (MANDATORY):
- ⚠️ WARNING at 500 lines - consider splitting
- 🔴 CRITICAL at 800+ lines - MUST split before continuing
- ✅ TARGET: 200-400 lines per file
- ❌ FORBIDDEN: Files > 1,000 lines

Added refactoring examples:
- parser.zig: 1,402 → 157 lines (split into 4 modules)
- codegen/classes.zig: 1,029 → 14 lines (split into 5 modules)
- Proven pattern: All modules now < 500 lines

Pure Zig Architecture (NO Python Runtime):
- ❌ REMOVED: Python FFI via python.zig
- ❌ FORBIDDEN: Python interpreter calls, Python C API
- ✅ REQUIRED: Pure Zig compilation only
- ✅ ALLOWED: Direct C interop via @cImport (for BLAS/etc)

Verification:
- Binary must have zero Python symbols
- No libpython dependencies
- All compilation in pure Zig

This prevents:
1. Files growing > 1,000 lines again
2. Python FFI creeping back in
3. Multi-agent conflicts from giant files

Updated file structure section to reflect current modular state.
